retry_reason: Failure indicates the namespace object is dying/dead (refcount zero). Retrying will not revive the object. For init ns, the path never fails.
contract_summary: Conditional get-wrapper that acquires a reference on a namespace (`struct ns_common`). For the initial (init) namespace, the function always succeeds without actually incrementing any refcount because it is permanently alive. For other namespaces, it calls refcount_inc_not_zero to increment the reference count only if it is non-zero (i.e., the object is still alive). Returns true if the caller now holds a reference that must later be released with a corresponding put (e.g., __ns_ref_put). Returns false if the namespace is dead; the caller must not use the pointer after failure. The caller must check the return value.

judge.reason: Primary purpose is to acquire a reference on a namespace object. VFS_WARN_ON_ONCE checks are debugging assertions, not core logic. The only substantive operation is refcount_inc_not_zero (or a no-op for init ns).

check.reason: The refcount_inc_not_zero call on &ns->__ns_ref correctly acquires a reference on the ns object passed as parameter. The access path ns.__ns_ref.refs and type chain ns_common--refcount_t--atomic_t are consistent with the callee's member path r.refs and the struct definitions. The segment count (3 segments, 3 types) matches. The critical variable is parameter 1 (ns), as the refcount is taken on that object and the function returns success/failure.
